Stricken language would be deleted from and underlined language would be added to present law. Act 2 of the First Extraordinary Session *JLL442* 04/30/2026 11:37:28 AM JLL442 State of Arkansas

Call Item 1 1 95th General Assembly A Bill

2 First Extraordinary Session, 2026

SENATE BILL 1 3

4 By: Senators J. Dismang, J. Boyd, J. Bryant, Caldwell, A. Clark, Crowell, B. Davis, Dees, J. Dotson, J. 5 English, Flippo, Gilmore, K. Hammer, Hester, Hickey, Hill, Irvin, B. Johnson, M. Johnson, B. King, M. 6 McKee, J. Payton, C. Penzo, J. Petty, Rice, Simon, Stone, D. Sullivan, D. Wallace 7 By: Representative Eaves 8

9 For An Act To Be Entitled 10 AN ACT TO REDUCE INCOME TAXES; TO REDUCE THE INCOME 11 TAX RATES FOR INDIVIDUALS, TRUSTS, ESTATES, AND 12 CORPORATIONS; AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES. 13

14

15 Subtitle 16 TO REDUCE THE INCOME TAX RATES FOR 17 INDIVIDUALS, TRUSTS, ESTATES, AND 18 CORPORATIONS. 19

20

21 BE IT ENACTED BY THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY OF THE STATE OF ARKANSAS: 22

23

SECTION 1. Arkansas Code § 26-51-201(a)(4), concerning the income tax 24 imposed on individuals, trusts, and estates, is amended to read as follows: 25

(4) For tax years beginning on or after January 1, 2026: 26

(A) Every resident, individual, trust, or estate having 27 net income less than or equal to ninety-four thousand seven hundred dollars 28 ($94,700) shall determine the amount of income tax due under this subsection 29 in accordance with the table set forth below: 30 From

(5) The tables set forth in subdivisions (a)(1)-(3) (a)(1)-(4) 9 of this section shall be adjusted annually in accordance with the method set 10 forth in subsection (d) of this section. 11

12

SECTION 2. Arkansas Code § 26-51-205(a), concerning the income tax 13 levied on domestic corporations, is amended to add an additional subdivision 14 to read as follows: 15

(6) For tax years beginning on or after January 1, 2027, every 16 corporation organized under the laws of this state shall pay annually an 17 income tax with respect to carrying on or doing business on the entire net 18 income of the corporation, as now defined by the laws of this state, received 19 by the corporation during the income year, on the following basis: 20

(A) On the first three thousand dollars ($3,000) of net 21 income or any part thereof, one percent (1%); 22

(B) On the next three thousand dollars ($3,000) of net 23 income or any part thereof, two percent (2%); 24

(C) On the next five thousand dollars ($5,000) of net 25 income or any part thereof, three percent (3%); and 26

(D) On net income exceeding eleven thousand dollars 27 ($11,000), four and one-tenths percent (4.1%). 28

29

SECTION 3. Arkansas Code § 26-51-205(b), concerning the income tax 30 levied on foreign corporations, is amended to add an additional subdivision 31 to read as follows: 32

(6) For tax years beginning on or after January 1, 2027, every 33 foreign corporation doing business within the jurisdiction of this state 34 shall pay annually an income tax on the proportion of its entire net income 35 as now defined by the income tax laws of this state, on the following basis: 36

SB1

4 04/30/2026 11:37:28 AM JLL442

(A) On the first three thousand dollars ($3,000) of net 1 income or any part thereof, one percent (1%); 2

(B) On the next three thousand dollars ($3,000) of net 3 income or any part thereof, two percent (2%); 4

(C) On the next five thousand dollars ($5,000) of net 5 income or any part thereof, three percent (3%); and 6

(D) On net income exceeding eleven thousand dollars 7 ($11,000), four and one-tenths percent (4.1%). 8

9

heading: Arkansas Act 2 of 2026 — income tax rates

metadata.source_note: Complete four-page enacted Act 2 of the 2026 First Extraordinary Session. Section 1 amends Ark. Code § 26-51-201(a)(4) for tax years beginning on or after January 1, 2026, including both rate schedules and the complete $94,701-$97,600 bracket-adjustment table. Sections 2 and 3 add the domestic- and foreign-corporation schedules in § 26-51-205(a)(6) and (b)(6) for tax years beginning on or after January 1, 2027. The Legislature bill page identifies SB1 as enacted Act 2 and the PDF records approval on May 6, 2026.
